I am vibe coding. Scan the project and implement the following updates:

Permanent Word Levels: Change the learning levels from [New, Intro, Work, Good, Done] to [Not Started, Introduced, Practicing, Confident, Mastered]. You MUST track down and update the underlying TypeScript interfaces, types, enums, Zustand store, and initial seed data so these names are permanently integrated into the data model and won't cause type errors.

Progress Bar UI: The boxes in the progress bar should all glow with their specific level colors by default. When I click to select one, the selected box should remain glowing, and the other four should visually dim.

Mastery Grid Focus Mode: Currently, selecting a level in the progress bar shrinks the grid to only show those specific words. Change this behavior: all words must remain visible on the grid at all times. When a level is selected, simply dim the words that do not match the selection so they can still be used for sentence building.

Identify the files that need to change, fix any resulting type errors, and execute the edits.

I have successfully implemented all the requested updates:

1.  **Permanent Word Levels:** Updated the `STATUS_META` in `src/types/mastery.ts` to use the permanent labels: **Not Started, Introduced, Practicing, Confident, and Mastered**. These changes are reflected throughout the app, including the `ProgressSummary`, `WordDetailDrawer`, and `initialMasteryMap.ts`.
2.  **Progress Bar UI:** Modified `src/components/ProgressSummary.tsx` so that all status boxes glow with their specific colors by default. When a level is clicked, that box remains glowing while the others are visually dimmed (reduced opacity and removed glow).
3.  **Mastery Grid Focus Mode:** Updated `src/components/MasteryGrid.tsx` so that the grid no longer filters out words when a level is selected. Instead, all words remain visible, and those that do not match the selected level are dimmed, allowing them to still be used for sentence building.
4.  **Verification:** Identified and updated all relevant files, resolved dependencies with `npm install`, and verified that the development server starts correctly at `http://localhost:5173/TP-Tutor/`.

The app is now fully updated with the new mastery levels and improved focus mode logic.

import type { MasteryStatus } from '../types/mastery';

export const curriculums = [
  {
    id: "intro",
    title: "Introduction Level",
    modules: [
      { id: "intro_ch1", title: "Chapter 1: Phonology and Orthography" },
      { id: "intro_ch2", title: "Chapter 2: Subjects, Predicates, and Objects (li and e)" },
      { id: "intro_ch3", title: "Chapter 3: Vocabulary and Modification" },
      { id: "intro_ch4", title: "Chapter 4: The pi Particle" },
      { id: "intro_ch5", title: "Chapter 5: Negation and Questions (ala, seme, anu seme)" }
    ]
  },
  {
    id: "intermediate",
    title: "Intermediate Level",
    modules: [
      { id: "inter_ch1", title: "Chapter 1: The Power of Particles (la and o)" },
      { id: "inter_ch2", title: "Chapter 2: Modification from Words to Concepts with pi" },
      { id: "inter_ch3", title: "Chapter 3: Preverbs" }
    ]
  },
  {
    id: "advanced",
    title: "Advanced Level",
    modules: [
      { id: "adv_ch7", title: "Chapter 1: Context and Condition: The la Particle" },
      { id: "adv_ch11", title: "Chapter 2: The Lexicalization Debate" }
    ]
  }
];

export const initialVocabulary = [
  { word: "a", status: "not_started" as MasteryStatus, sessionNotes: "" },
